title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 513.637 - Enforcement of judgments — attorney general, duties — authority to enter into reciprocal agreements with other jurisdictions
text:
513.637.  Enforcement of judgments — attorney general, duties — authority to enter into reciprocal agreements with other jurisdictions. — 1.  Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a valid judgment rendered by a court of a jurisdiction having a law substantially similar to sections 513.600 to 513.645 will be recognized and enforced by the courts of this state to the extent that a judgment rendered by a court of this state pursuant to sections 513.600 to 513.645 would be enforced in such other jurisdiction.
  2.  The attorney general is hereby authorized to enter into reciprocal agreements with the attorney general or chief prosecuting attorney of any jurisdiction having a law substantially similar to sections 513.600 to 513.645 so as to further the purposes of sections 513.600 to 513.645.
(L. 1986 S.B. 450 § 15) 
Effective 3-17-86
